summary: Clear enough sound‑board capture of a vibrant June 8 1992 show, highlighted by scorching Loser, soaring So Many Roads and a stellar Stella Blue.
review: The performance is widely praised for its high energy jams – an intense Loser solo, a frenzied So Many Roads, a punchy Dark Star and a beautiful Stella Blue – with most fans calling it one of the best shows of the summer ’92 run. The sound‑board source is generally clear, though a few reviewers note muffled percussion and vocals that sit too far forward in the mix.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews (over 20) give 4‑5 stars and highlight many standout songs; recording quality is described as clear with only minor mix issues, yielding a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall clear and balanced; occasional muffled percussion, vocals pushed forward, and tape‑flip fade‑ins noted, but the guitar and overall mix are solid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set strong but a bit short; second set builds momentum with extended jams and smoother transitions, delivering an engaging, well‑paced two‑hour experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Intense solo on Loser, frenzied vocal work on So Many Roads, consistently active guitar throughout; vocals sometimes too forward in the mix.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mixed remarks – some praise for his bass lines and standout moments, but one reviewer called him "bombing" during a Stella.
band_member_comments.Bob: Recognized for delivering a powerful anthem (U.S. Blues) and solid rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent absent (missed), Vince praised in later comments; keyboards generally supportive without dominating.
band_member_comments.Drums: Post‑drum segment highlighted as awesome; Bill and Mickey noted as playing at their best on Stella Blue.
